They had already made all the preparations possible. After learning from "Two Under One Roof" today, they will open seven stream rooms tomorrow, with each one connecting to each other. When the time comes, they could use the popularity rankings to attract fans and make them give gold for views.

He believed that "Heartbeat" would become one of the biggest money-making machines at Muyao Network this year!

As for "Two Under One Roof", everyone was only interested right now because of Shi Sui. After they get sick of him, wouldn't they all flock to "Heartbeat"?

"Director Chen, the signal has returned for "Two Under One Roof", notifying that they will begin streaming…" The assistant reported, trembling.

Director Chen said in disdain, "If they keep adjusting equipment and interrupting their signals, how can any audience bear this? They must be losing viewers at a rapid rate…"

The assistant: "Eh… Director Chen, they… they haven't lost any views; it's actually going up!"

Director Chen: ???

What happened to ditching the show??

chapters.title: Chapter 48 - Can You Have Some Shame
chapters.body:
Chapter 48 - Can You Have Some Shame

The third broadcast of "Two Under One Roof" on the first day began.

The camera started with… ground pot stewed chicken*.

The camera had zoomed in first on the marinated chicken, and the audience heard Li Jianyu stifling his laughter through the radio equipment.

"This is Little Yellow in our chicken coop. Cause of death: Too fat. He left us in peace. Let's remember him and thank Director Yan for the guidance. Thank you for your hard work; your sword-wielding skills are not rusty at all!"

Live comments:

——[First-Blood !]

—[First Blood!]

—[Whoever mentioned first blood, did you forget about the little shrimps from lunch?]

—[Crayfish: Do you remember that year's apricot blossom rain this year? It was the wrong payment after all]

—[??? wait, are you a romance show or a culinary show]

Everyone's questions continued, but they were intrigued nonetheless. Even some people treated it like a mukbang and yelled to their parents, "Mom, I want to eat stewed chicken tonight". Others also opened their takeout software to call some for themselves.

The camera panned to Yan Nai, who was burning the pot.

—[My god, he's so cute!!]

—[Didi, stop blowing at the fire. Come, kiss me!]

—[Ahhhhh he's not a fireman, he's a fireboy!]

—[The one who said "fireboy", are you trying to kill me with laughter so you could inherit my takeout?]

As far as Yan Zhenhua was concerned, the well-known director wasn't idle either. He was actually… peeling garlic?!

He hummed a small tune as he peeled the garlic in a jolly mood, then seriously said to the camera, "Men must have work to do. You cannot aspire to be a lazy man; lazy men will be beaten by their mothers-in-law."

Live comments:

—[Makes sense he's a cultural figure; this irony is good]

—[Director Yan is so cute; he's so sarcastic and full of laughing points]

—[Ah ah ah he's coming! Shi Yingdi is here! Wait, what's that thing he's holding in his hand? A chopping board??]

—[The Vase is holding the vegetable knife? What's going on? I'm starting to pinch myself. How come two people holding a cutting board and a vegetable knife can walk like models on the runway??]

—[Why do I feel like these two feel like…a couple… ]

—[ Get out get out, I reject CP. I'm taking away my Shi Yingdi. We're not meeting with you]

Shi Sui was indeed carrying a chopping board used for cutting vegetables. The board in the small kitchen was way too small, so he had helped Xiang Yi bring the large one from the big kitchen.

Xiang Yi was carrying the vegetable knife and some seasoning.

She lifted the knife and cut the whole chicken into pieces.

Peeled garlic, sliced ginger, chopped up green onions and chili were prepared. After the fire was lit, oil was added into the pot. Garlic, ginger, green onion, and green peppers were added into the wok, as well as red peppers and the secret recipe chilli sauce.

The earthen furnace was on high heat and immediately a pungent aroma could be smelled.

After frying the seasoning, she added some water and let it simmer.

Xiang Yi had also prepared some filling sides. Shiitake mushrooms, bean sprouts, potatoes, bamboo shoot tips, dried beans, etc;. She then put them in at the most appropriate time.

She had left the flour for the naan to rise in advance, pressing them into evenly shaped strips before sticking them around the earthen furnace pot, allowing the chicken broth and water vapor to soak it thoroughly.

If someone else had to be in charge of this procedure, they would have been a disgruntled mess. Yet, Xiang Yi had completed it methodically and not in a hurry.

In front of their screens, many audience members felt respectful from the bottom of their hearts. Although the Vase Fairy was a bit too dramatic and a little dumb, her cooking skills was still very admirable and pleasing to watch…

Yan Nai swallowed and couldn't resist asking, "Sister Xiang Yi, what's for breakfast tomorrow?"

Xiang Yi thought about it. "Tomorrow, I'll go to my barn to fetch some fresh milk. How about some fried milk?"

The live comments:

—[I feel awkward for the Vase Fairy even through the screen…]

—[Your farm? Sister, can we please have some shame? I'm begging you]

Translator's note:

* Despite being called ground pot, this famous dish from Xuzhou region primarily differs in its use of wood fire to cook stewed chicken.

chapters.title: Chapter 49 - Green Tea Cat
chapters.body:
Chapter 49 - Green Tea Cat

The comments once again went into upheaval because of what Xiang Yi had said.

Someone uttered the meek sentence [My college classmate was at the live shooting, and I hear that it really is Xiang Yi's home… She was the one who offered it to be the film location…]

Unfortunately, there were too many comments and this particular one disappeared in the blink of an eye, like a drop of water submerged into the sea without even a splash.

The earthen pot chicken has to be simmered for a while before the flavour could get in. The Yan father and son continued to gaze at the pot with sparkling eyes. Even when Xiang Yi repeatedly said that even if there wasn't fire, the temperature inside the furnace was enough to maintain a simmer, the two would not stop their work.

An elder and a youngster were very clever in their greed.

"Is there any sour plum soup leftover from lunch?" Yan Zhenhua swallowed. "This chicken will be salty if we eat too much, right?"

Yan Nai: "Cola is good, too. With some ice cubes."

Xiang Yi was delightfully entertained, her eyes forming into half moons. "We have both sour plum soup and cola, and there's also some plum wine in the house from before as well. Does Director Yan want to try it?"

Yan Zhenhua's eyes lit up. "You know how to brew? Then I'm really in for it today."

"Not really, plum wine is quite simple to brew," Xiang Yi said modestly as she stood up to fetch the wine.

Tong Tong—or the wittle tiger—had taken a bath and cleaned his fur, becoming soft and cute. He had evolved into a cute and loving little kitty, walking around lazily as if he had come to inspect the territory.

As soon as he entered, he caught the eye of several viewers.

Li Jianyu acted as a narrator, explaining: "This is the cat we found this afternoon. His name is Xiao Naohu* (Wittle Tiger)."

—[What a cute cat I could kill ten by sitting on them]

—[Baby, what's your favorite color of rucksack??]

—[Can I ask where you found the cat? I'm eagerly awaiting]

The cameraman generously took a few closeup shots of Xiao Naohu. The arrogant and haughty cat walked around on his short legs until it stopped at Shi Sui's feet.

"Meow?"

Xiao Naohu tilted his head curiously, feeling that Shi Sui had a familiar aura, as if it was someone he knew.

Could it be… He's also a world traveler?

Impossible.

Before he was reborn, he had checked the information of this world, and there was only Xiang Yi.

Ah, he didn't think his head would hurt so much.

Shi Sui lowered his peachy eyes as he picked up the cat by the nape of his neck, holding him in his arms as he gently stroked at it a few times.

The man and cat met each other's gazes, and the scene was beautiful and harmonious.

—[Ahhh ah ahhh I want to be held by gege, too]

—[I'm so sour, I'm so sour! I'm not even good enough to be a cat]

—[Shi Yingdi is so gentle; what a nice man. I'm going to nose bleed]

Xiao Naohu had been enjoying being pampered by humans until he heard the sound of footsteps approaching. The cat's ears trembled and suddenly screamed: "Meow!"

Xiang Yi heard his cry and hurried over. She put down the tray with drinks, pulling Xiao Naohu from Shi Sui as she stared at him.

Xiao Naohu began to rub against her in her arms, purring as if he'd been wronged by heaven itself.

Xiang Yi clutched him as she retreated backwards. "Senior, please don't bully him."

An explicable thought came to his mind:

'Is a cat more important than me?'

In the stream.

—[Is this the legendary green… green tea cat??]

—[Is this an illusion? Is Xiao Naohu trying to fight for attention?!!]

—[Why do I see in my Shi Yingdi's eyes the feeling of being wronged??]

Shi Sui's thin lips quirked as he tried to explain. Xiao Naohu suddenly began to meow again as Xiang Yi coaxed softly. "Be good, I'll take you to the new cat bed, okay?"

Xiao Naohu: "Miao~"

Shi Sui: "…"

The cat was really more important than him.

*Translator's Note: Xiao Naohu is basically a cuter way of saying 'xiao lao hu', which means little tiger. Since this is officially his name now, we can address him by Xiao Naohu rather than wittle tiger.

chapters.title: Chapter 50 - Large Scale Face Slapping 1
chapters.body:
Chapter 50 - Large Scale Face Slapping 1

The cat bed had been brought in by Xiang Feng's people, shaped like a red ingot. It was fluffy and soft, and when Xiao Naofu jumped into it, he couldn't help but roll around happily.

It seems like Xiang Yi spoiled him quite heavily.

As for that man with the peach blossom eyes*, 'Hmph. Don't think you could steal Xiang Yi's adoration just because you are handsome!'

But why did he have such a familiar aura? If given the chance, he would definitely check it out carefully.

Under the eager anticipation of the Yan father and son, mealtime finally began.

Yan Zhenhua had scooped the dishes himself after finding the largest bucket in the kitchen. The netizens in the live comments were all saying how down to earth this famous director was.

They took out some naan but left the others to remain heated inside the pot.

The meal was accompanied by exquisite side dishes, which included a fresh salad Xiang Yi had tossed together as well as some pickled kimchi and radish.

"Okay, let's eat." As Yan Zhenhua finished speaking, he shamelessly picked up a piece of chicken for himself.

The chicken had been free-range and grass fed, so its meat was firm and delicious. It was even more fragrant when stewed in the ground pot. Aside from the pepper, they didn't add any additional seasoning, which amplified its freshness.

Matched with a sip of plum wine, the fresh and intense flavor of the fruit provided a refreshing cleanser for the palette. It was unbeatable!

Shi Sui picked up some vegetables to taste. Regardless if it was the bamboo shoots, or the dried green beans, they had all been soaked in the chicken broth. It was delicious but not greasy, and a hint of spice would blossom on one's tongue without overwhelming the palette. He particularly loved the mushrooms, which had a fresh and slippery taste, and even found it more appetizing than the chicken.

Yan Nai was another matter. As inhaling mouthfuls of naan bread after meat after vegetables, he had actually learned to rip apart the naan to fill it with veggies and meat, as if he were creating a hamburger.

—[This is a f*cking culinary show!! /smile /smile]

—[I'm going crazy. I've been eating along with this stream not only while I am cooking, but also while I'm eating. My stomach has become round!]

—[Where's the drama? Where's the fighting? Where is the romance? How come there's no content aside from eating? Fine, fine; I'll stop hating. Gonna go make some instant noodles]

The crew had realized that whenever it was mealtime, the comment sections and the live comments would be unexpectedly harmonious…

Even Xiang Yi managed to escape the toxicity…

Li Jianyu's expression suddenly changed from trance to sudden realization.

He finally understood the useful reason behind why Daddy Shi had recommended Xiang Yi!

There was no romance there!

He just wanted a good meal!

Moreover, Xiang Yi's culinary skills were quite excellent. She might be able to cleanse herself of her rumors, although gaining fans was still out of the question. But at least she could reduce her anti-fans.

The audience's attention was focused on the dining table. Only the people in Xiang Yi's broadcast room could see that she did not join them at the table but had separated some ground pot chicken and veggies into small plates, putting them all in a tray.

The camera then panned to Xiang Yi as she entered the manor, while the cameraman waited outside.

The audience: ???

—[For the manager? Why can't that be filmed?]

—[The manager probably doesn't care if he's filmed, but if it was another random person, then they probably won't want to be filmed]

Suddenly, everyone understood. After entering the automated door with her facial recognition, a slender silhouette of a male appeared for a brief instant before the camera.

The audience: !!!

—[Her manager isn't that tall. It's not him!]

—[Isn't the vase fairy desperately clinging onto Shi Yingdo? But why is she delivering food for another man behind his back?! ]

—[I have a bold thought, what if it's her sugar daddy… ]

*Translator's note: Peach blossom eyes refer to a particular shape of eye that is considered conventionally attractive, the kind that form half moons when they smile. They are also referred to as 'bedroom eyes' occasionally.

chapters.title: Chapter 51 - Large Scale Face Slapping 2
chapters.body:
Chapter 51 - Large Scale Face Slapping 2

One of the unsolved mysteries of the world was probably the speed at which rumors spread.

Soon, Xiang Yi's broadcast room was filled with a curious crowd.

—[Where's the sugar daddy?]

—[Aiyah, why is it so dark? didn't someone say the Vase Fairy was secretly meeting a wild man on a variety show?]

—[Why is she being so secretive? Something is definitely off! This kind of scheming woman is not even worthy of carrying our Shi Yingdi's shoes!]

—[Sister Yi is just delivering food. You guys keep talking about a sugar daddy, but it seems to me like you're just going crazy wanting to find one for yourself]

—[Hehe sorry, our sister Yi is beautiful, and your Shi Yingdi seems very satisfied from every meal. If you don't think he's compatible, tell him to stop eating~]

But in reality—

Inside the living room, Xiang Feng burst forward like a rabid dog, showing the palms of his hands to Xiang Yi. "Look, I washed my hands!"

The Xiang family habitually kept a low profile, and although Xiang Feng was the CEO of an entertainment company, he seldom showed his face.

Even if the artists on variety shows were very popular, he was too lazy to bother. He had only stayed because he wanted to eat his niece's food.

"Mhmm. Go ahead."

"Are you sure it's okay for you to miss your stream?" Xiang Feng asked as he ate, gasping for breath through the steaming food. "I heard the results were pretty good. You even gained a hundred thousand or so new followers on Weibo."

Xiang Yi's body was still recuperating, and she couldn't eat too much greasy food. In addition, her digestive system wasn't very good; hence, her dinner was only a bowl of millet porridge.

Xiao Naofu had been eating his own special cat food on the side, which was a few peeled shrimps and a clump of pumpkin.

The girl's motions as she ate her porridge were unhurried as she calmly spoke. "Nobody will notice I'm gone."

Xiang Feng thought about it and agreed. Aside from when she was cooking, his little niece didn't have much presence on the variety show.

He had thought it was because she didn't have a variety personality; but listening to her tone, there was something else behind it.

As everyone knows, there are way too many bored, melon-eating gossips on the net these days. The comment section of her personal live broadcast had been filled with conflicting opinions, gossip, and chatter. It was quite lively.

The number of viewers even reached an all-time high.

"You're really… planning to leave the industry? But back in the day when you wanted to enter the industry, you even signed a contract with Xiang Chen…"

Xiang Chen was Xiang Yi's eldest brother, and his law firm was one of the top grossing offices in China. His personal portfolio and business abilities were top-notch in the industry, too.

Xiang Yi paused her movements for a while, her expression rather helpless. "What contract?"

"I'm not too sure about the specifics, but there were a lot of loose ends and odd clauses. There was probably something along the lines of not letting people know your relationship with the Xiang family and that you can no longer collect allowance from the family." Xiang Feng tasted both the sour plum soup and the plum wine. He preferred the sweet and sour taste of the soup rather than the wine and picked up the glass and gulped down a few mouthfuls.

Xiang Yi frowned. Why would such a notable lawyer like her brother sign such an imm.a.t.u.r.e contract with her…

Could it be… her eldest brother knew that she hadn't been herself last year?!

"Little uncle, where is my eldest brother now?"

"He's overseas right now on an IPO case. He's kind of busy and probably won't be back for at least another month."

Xiang Yi wanted to video call, but she resisted the urge.

This kind of thing was better discussed in person.

Xiang Feng was fast and finished his food quickly, waving Xiang Yi off to go back to her stream.

Xiang Yi returned to the pavilion with a heavy heart.

The three guys were quite full from the food already.

Yan Zhenhua had been urging Shi Sui to perform, while Shi Sui sat lazily with his legs crossed on the floor, smiling gently without agreeing or rejecting his request.

The dim light of the pavilion glazed his face with a gentle and warm tone. After seeing Xiang Yi, he spoke in a low and raspy voice. "Xiang Yi, he's bullying me."

Matched with his helpless expression, he looked three points wronged, three points helpless, and four points pitiful…

//Shisui: Hey, little cat, if you want to compete in acting, I'm a professional, okay?

//Goodnight~

chapters.title: Chapter 52 - Large Scale Face Slapping 3
chapters.body:
Chapter 52 - Large Scale Face Slapping 3

Xiang Yi: "…"

Was zaizai* wronged?

The next moment, she pulled out a piece of candy from her pocket and gently said, "Be good. Don't cry."

The several audience members in the stream: ???

Li Jianyu and Ah Nan: ???

The Yan father and son, who were still s.u.c.k.i.n.g on the chicken bones: ???

'Woman, you are in dangerous territory here!'

'Are you trying to coax Shi Yingdi like a child?!!!'

Unfortunately—

Shi Sui's peach blossom eyes fell on the palm of Xiang Yi's hands. The girl was small and had small hands that were soft and fair with clear palm prints. Lying quietly on it was a piece of white chocolate with strawberry filling.

"For me?"

Xiang Yi's expression was stiff. Sh*t. She had promised Ah Nan to keep her distance from him….

But she was used to coaxing people and couldn't control her own movements, or her mouth for that matter.

The deed was done, and it would seem hypocritical to deny it at this moment. "This tastes really good, try it. Maybe you'll feel better."

In the deathly silence, there was the sound of a 'tear'. Xiang Yi wanted to stick the last nail in the coffin and coax her zaizai to the end. She had ripped open the candy wrapper…

The audience: !!!

'Woman, don't you think your actions are a little too rehearsed?!'

Shi Sui paused for a moment then received the candy. He bit into it lightly. The sweet and sour strawberry blended with the rich chocolate and bloomed at the tip of his tongue.

He curled his lips. "Mhm. It is pretty good."

The man smiled casually, bringing his surroundings to life.

It turns out that some people on this planet need not fight for the center position.

Because regardless of where Shi Sui was, there the spotlight would be.

Inside the stream, Shi Sui's fans, the Shisui sisters, were about to go crazy.

—[Of course! The Vase Fairy is trying to seduce our gege!]

—[Ah shibal! Xiang Yi is such a deceptive woman; she's too skilled. Why doesn't she just publish a seductress guide?]

— [QQ our Shi Sui zaizai is too easily fooled. He looks so happy with just one piece of candy. Your mommy won't allow it!]

Yan Zhenhua felt the atmosphere had become a bit pink and bubbly and coughed abruptly. Taking the lead, he said, "Xiang Yi ah, do you have any talents?"

Ah Nan's world turned dark for a moment.

'Director Yan, Director Yan, are you trying to save the scene or destroy it?!'

'You, sir, probably have never seen Xiang Yi's acting skills or her dramas. It literally burns your eyes. So what if she wasn't talented? She didn't even bother to read over the script; she just counted 1234 and waited for her voice to be dubbed.'

Later, she went the variety show star route. She didn't know how to sing, how to dance, or how to respond to jokes; she would cry if she was even just a bit tired. If she was slightly unhappy, she'd cry; if she even felt slightly wronged, she would cry.

…It was a bit cruel to say she was a tumor to the industry, but this was indeed the situation.

He tugged on Li Jianyu's sleeve, shaking both his hands in protest. He wanted to beg her to help him out, but instead he heard her say, "Does an instrument count? I know a little."

Yan Zhenhua bellowed with laughter. "Of course."

Xiang Yi did not linger and boldly walked off. "I'll go grab it."

After she left the room, Yan Zhenhua began to chat with the audience about an interesting story. "Didn't Shi Sui film a TV series a while ago? Yes, the one that won him the Emperor Award. Of course, there was a banquet and a fighting plot that was a test of instrument playing skills. Most actors didn't know how to play instruments, but if you want to create a realistic and beautiful performance, hehe… Do you see where I'm going with this?"

Yan Zhenhua: "When Xiang Yi finishes performing later, everyone, don't forget to show your support. Let's not hurt the little girl's feelings!"

Yan Nai responded in a straightforward manner. "What if she sucks?"

Yan Zhenhua refrained from rolling his eyes. 'Why did I give birth to such a blunt and honest son! When I was your age, I'd written about half a meter worth of love letters to your mom already!'

Translator's Note:

* Zaizai is an alternative term for baby, but in an endearing way which Xiang Yi had adapted for Shi Sui

chapters.title: Chapter 53 - Large Scale Face Slapping 4
chapters.body:
Chapter 53 - Large Scale Face Slapping 4

"You…" Yan Zhenhua ground his teeth. "Just remember one thing, Xiang Yi's performance is top-notch in the world! Don't forget to praise her!"

Yan Nai scratched his head. "How? Do I just… force it?"

Shi Sui: "I suggest turning off your mic."

In a moment:

—[If didi continues like this, he won't find a girlfriend 23333]

—[Ahahahah lmao, I had no idea Shi Yingdi was this funny]

—[Director Yan's face looks like this emoji: CantBearToLook.jpg]

As they spoke, Xiang Yi came out with an instrument.

At first glance, the musical instrument looked like an erhu that has wooden head, neck, and body parts. The redwood of the instrument seemed to be antique, and it gave the whole instrument a unique charm.

Xiang Yi's haters in the stream immediately began to discuss.

—[I thought it was going to be so badass. But this? This tacky, ugly thing??]

—[Is she poisonous? Isn't this the shamisen* from Japan? No wonder she is the Vase Fairy: she can't do anything she tries at, but she's number one at selling out to other countries]

—[This seems very difficult to play. Is she not aware of her own capabilities? She coulda just performed a harmonica or played the piano with her thumb. Why create such a scene just to lose your own face?]

The next second.

Yan Zhenhua asked in surprise, "This is… a sanxian?"

In his spare time, Yan Zhenhua liked to visit teahouses and listen to local opera. This instrument looked familiar to him, but he wasn't a hundred-percent sure.

Xiang Yi mumbled a quick "mhmm". "Are there any particular songs you'd like to hear?"

Yan Zhenhua knew of a few tunes, but he really couldn't name one on the spot. Smiling, he said, "Anything is okay, just do your best."

"Please bear with me."

As her voice fell, the girl's slender fingers began to press on the strings.

A traditional tune began to sound; the unique strong yet clear sound of the sanxian suddenly pulling people into a smoky world.

Yan Zhenhua initially thought that Xiang Yi had only casually learned a few notes, but after listening to a short verse, he realized that this doll was not your average skilled player!

The level of the performers at the teahouse differed between master and junior apprentice, and the difference was quite obvious. But Xiang Yi's level of mastery was on par with those skilled masters!

Yan Nai had stopped s.u.c.k.i.n.g on his chicken bone as he stared at Xiang Yi in allure.

When she was serious, it was like she came with her own luminous filter that was sparkling like a diamond…

Yan Nai once again felt his ears turn red.

'Our sweet dear is really this talented??'

Inside the stream, the netizens also began to spam out question marks.

—[Whoever said this was a shamisen is an idiot. This is the traditional sanxian from our country!]

—[It takes a thousand days to learn the pipa, a hundred days to learn the zither, but half a lifetime for the sanxian. The sanxian is very difficult to learn; this girl has a few tricks up her sleeves]

—[Holy f*ck, I'm watching the stream in my yard, and as soon as the Vase Fairy began to play, my grandparents ran out from their weather report to compliment this girl on her skills!]

—[This is amazing; this is the feeling of walking around the Hutongs in the capital with a birdcage, getting tired, and sitting down on a rocking chair under the shade of a tree. Teasing birds and drinking tea, I could almost see the scenery through her sound. This is damn amazing! You guys keep calling her a Vase Fairy when she's obviously a treasure girl*! I'm a fan, I'm a fan!!!]

The netizens who had been scolding her suddenly changed their expressions, each one more surprised than the next.

This really was a large scale face slapping…

Only this time, it wasn't Xiang Yi slapping her own face but fiercely slapping those uncultured frogs who live in wells!

Translator's Note:

* Shamisen is a string instrument from Japan, making up of three strings and modelling itself after the Chinese lute instrument, sanxian. Xiang Yi plays the sanxian.

* 'Treasure Girl' is a trending online term used to describe an idol who had so much dirt on her that she could not be dug out but has a lot of nice qualities embedded deeper within her that have yet to be discovered, much like treasure.

chapters.title: Chapter 54 - Untimely Shi Yi
chapters.body:
Chapter 54 - Untimely Shi Yi

Halfway through the performance, the girl's string plucking patterns changed from a folk song into an iron horse on a frozen river. The sound of the sanxian suddenly became distant yet close at the same time, going fast and slow, while her fingering technique was dazzling. Soon, the tune revealed a simple desolation and intensity, but it was able to ignite the deepest blood in people's hearts.

—[I'm on my knees in front of this master… Although I don't understand the sanxian, but these finger techniques are something an uncoordinated person like me could never learn]

—[Music can really convey emotions without words. You can really hear the magnificence of the wind and rain shaking the mountains and rivers during tumultuous times. Folk music forever!]

—[Whenever I see people say that the shamisen is the westernized version of the sanxian, I get really angry. Musical instruments shouldn't have a hierarchy to distinguish between high and low. Plus, the shamisen evolved from our sanxian. This sister's impromptu performance is the best slap in the face!]

After the climax, the sound of the sanxian changed again. The frequency gradually began to slow into a soothing little tune. It strongly contrasted with the previous melody, but it was not so abrupt that it felt unnatural.

When the last note fell, everyone in the pavilion was shocked. The comments in the stream were reduced to an all-time low since the show began.

A longing for more.

This was everyone's thought at the time.

Until—

Shi Sui's applause was the first to sound.

It wasn't that kind of shallow, surface applause, but a genuine appreciation from the heart.

They say that talented men are mesmerizing, so what does that make of talented women?

Yan Zhenhua followed in applause, becoming more and more satisfied with this 'future daughter-in-law'. "Good good good, it almost makes me want to buy a rocking chair and some tea."

Yan Nai, who was living half a wavelength behind everyone, unfortunately did not beat Shi Sui to applauding first. He also wasn't as good at praising people as his father was. It took him a long time to utter, "Sister, your sanxian skills are just as good as your cooking skills!"

Yan Zhenhua did not praise him often, so he hadn't acc.u.mulated any vocabulary in the field of flattery. It already churned at his stomach to utter such a phrase.

Xiang Yi smiled, but she did not take the praise to heart.

While she had been traveling through the universe, she had seen way too many masters. There were even people in these fairytale universes that practiced Taoism out of sheer joy.

In addition, she hadn't played in such a long time that her hands felt foreign.

She felt that she was only of average skill, and this was just for entertainment—not enough to perform on an actual stage.

But contrary to her beliefs, she had actually made a huge impact in the Chinese folk music scene that night!

After a leisurely recount, the guests all returned to their rooms as the stream came to an end.

However, the show's popularity did not diminish. For a while, news about "Two Under One Roof" could be found everywhere: in WeChat moments, on Weibo, online forums, and blogs.

In the middle of night, such a post appeared on the well-known gossip forum for the entertainment industry, [Melon].

The topic: [The Circ.u.mstance that Left Famous Actor Shi Yingdi Out in the Cold]

The header: First of all, I am a Shisui sister, and I have been a fan of his since his first movie.

As a fan, it's not hard to see that Shi Yingdi is a seemingly gentle person, but he has a good sense of measure in dealing with people and things. Let's focus on the main point. His good sense of measure is actually another form of expressing distance. The simplest example, he is not particularly known for having close friends in the industry. He's seldom in contact with his most supportive and wealthy fans.

For such a cold mountain, a god-tier man above all people, to be left out to dry N times by one of the most famous crybaby vases in entertainment history…

Then, she had peeled crayfish for Director Yan and even her manager, but she didn't peel any for our Shi Yingdi. Can this be tolerated??

Then because of a cat, she actually misunderstood Shi Yingdi! This is too much, right? Can this be tolerated???!

…Yes, I can tolerate everything above. AND I think these two have incredible CP vibes. It's too magical! SO. I plan to call them: [Untimely Shi Yi]* CP!

The post immediately received several responses.

—[Impractical, get lost! ]

—[Unimaginable, get lost!]

—[Not worth mentioning, get lost! ]

—[NO!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! ]

*Translator's Note: The CP name "Untimely Shi Yi" comes partly from the Shi in Shi Sui and Yi in Xiang Yi, both using the same spelling for "Shi Yi", which means "Time". It also just happens that both their names are synonymous and use the same characters as those in the proverb "Bu He Shi Yi", which essentially describes an inopportune moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 55 - A Disturbance In The Folk Music Circle 1
chapters.body:
Chapter 55 - A Disturbance In The Folk Music Circle 1

Some people opposed it, while others supported the pairing.

As the number of replies increased, a few 'outliers' emerged.

—[Abstinent film emperor vs beautiful vase, isn't this the cookie cutter entertainment circle romance?]

—[the moment these two appear together, all that's missing is a soft light filter to turn it into a scene from a romance drama. As someone with an appearance complex, I support this couple]

—[Forgive my straightforwardness, but I was home all weekend picking my feet, so I watched the entire stream. Xiang Yi knows how to cook and play the sanxian, and she's pretty and has a good personality. Where is the 'unworthiness' to date Shi Sui?]

One after another, fans came on and off the platform to tear away at the popularity of these posts, some even being transferred to Weibo's marketing accounts.

Because it came with a #ShiSui hashtag, the Shisui Sisters constantly saw these posts appearing on their Weibo feed. They were furious.

There's still a basic law if you want to create a scandal, okay?

Top-tier rumor-mongering could still be glorified as a marketing method.

But what right did this eighteenth-tier vase have to leech off Shi Yingdi's popularity?

The Shisui Sisters were mostly pretty m.a.t.u.r.e as they urged everyone to stay calm and ignore the Vase Fairy. However, some emotional fans had resorted to mocking and ridiculing Xiang Yi on her Weibo.

—[Want to create a dating rumor, are you worthy??]

—[F*ck, what is indisputable in this world? If you really didn't want to fight for it, then leave the cast. Without you, Two Under One Roof would be even better]

—[Our Gege has actual achievements like box office hits, awards, luxury endors.e.m.e.nts, and four major magazine covers. What do you have? Stir fry? That's laughable ]

—[How many women in the past had sacrificed their blood, sweat, and tears for women's suffrage to be equal to men? But you? Are you trying to take women back to the stove and become housewives? Is the value of a woman only reflected by their cooking?!]

The netizens watched the drama unfold with relish, sighing in unison. Shi Yingdi wasn't a top ranking 'fresh meat'*, but his fans were even more ferocious than that of an actual fresh meat.

The Vase Fairy was going to be ridiculed until she shut herself off. .

Maybe she won't even appear in tomorrow's streams.

But someone sent a peculiar post: "All things said, didn't the Vase Fairy say she was going to make fried milk tomorrow morning? I kind of want to see if it's tasty or not…"

The statement resonated with several people.

To be honest… If the Vase Fairy were to just cook and not try anything demonic, she would not actually be that annoying.

At this time, Xiang Yi's place in the hearts of most people was equivalent to that of a chef for "Two Under One Roof".

[A Grey Turtle Dove V]: I haven't posted on Weibo in a while, and several friends and fans have been concerned about my current situation. I'll explain it here: The last few days, my father has been in an ill state. We've visited several famous doctors to no avail; he is still exhausted. I don't think he has much time left.

He is a sanxian teacher at one of China's music conservatories. In recent years, fewer and fewer children have chosen to learn traditional music, and even fewer have chosen to study the sanxian. When he was seriously ill, he still tried his best although he wasn't able to lead his last few disciples well and was afraid that there would be no successors for this traditional art.

Today, he coincidentally heard Miss @XiangYiV's sanxian performance. In the last few days, because of his illness, he's become increasingly irritable and had no will to survive. However, he still asked me to replay it several times while listening to it calmly.

His comment: "Like pearls falling on jade, the string is like a whisper when the night is listening to the wind and rain, and the iron horse approaches on an icy lake. It takes a thousand days to learn the pipa, a hundred days for the zither, but half a lifetime for the sanxian. The skills I have honed over half my lifetime is not as good as this little girl. Sad, but gratifying, and worthy of congratulatory praise! The sanxian has a successor, and folk music has hope!"

Just now, he had agreed to undergo Western treatment and begin chemotherapy.

I have a lot of emotions, but I don't know where to start. I would like to express my deepest gratitude to Miss Xiang Yi.

As soon as this Weibo was posted, the Shisui Sisters, who had been on a roasting spree, now fell silent one by one.

Translator's Note:

* Fresh meat is the term used to describe young and 'fresh' looking individuals in the industry, usually idols.

chapters.title: Chapter 56 - A Disturbance In The Folk Music Circle 2
chapters.body:
Chapter 56 - A Disturbance In The Folk Music Circle 2

Some people weren't convinced, thinking some blogger was just trying to ride the trend. But when they saw his personal page, they were dumbfounded.

He had millions of followers, and their engagement rate was very high, too. He had over tens of thousands of comments on every post. Although he was an internet celebrity, he didn't fail to win several annual musician and producer awards each year. This person definitely had a strong influence in the industry.

Taking a closer look, his birth name was Ban Hui and was an honorary professor from a prestigious school. He had even given public lectures to world-class music schools as well…

In popular terms: this grey turtle dove* was a practiced master! And in the music industry, he was a master above masters!

The comment section instantly became lively. .

—[Heh, you guys are all uncultured noobs. Does the resume of Professor Ban scare you?]

—[I recommend searching up Professor Ban's father, Ban Jin. There's a surprise /cute /cute]

—[I'm in shock. So the famous master, Professor Ban, is actually speaking up for the Vase Fairy?]

Most of the Shisui Sisters kept their silence discreetly, but a small group of people were too deep in their inherent judgement toward Xiang Yi to act appropriately. They did not believe, nor were they willing to admit, that Xiang Yi was talented.

—[He probably got hacked. You guys are so gullible]

—[Interesting, she could be put on a pedestal for just strumming a random instrument a few times. How much money did the Vase Fairy spend on this endors.e.m.e.nt?]

—[Honestly, I didn't think she was that good. Although I don't understand, it felt pretty casual to me~ It was just fast strumming, that's all]

—[I don't know about a three string or a four string, what's so good about folk music? If it was so great, then why do all the kids choose to study piano or violin?]

These remarks were nothing when it came to disagreements between fans. At least, they hadn't greeted Xiang Yi's mother yet.

But something unexpected happened just then…

First was the Grey Turtle Dove responding to a few dozen replies:

"I wasn't hacked."

"I didn't take any money."

"Art is technique, but technique is not art. Miss Xiang Yi's performance was natural, at ease, and painted a picture with music. To me, her technique has reached the level of art."

"It's your personal preference to learn western music or traditional instruments. But why is it so difficult to recognize the excellence of traditional music while western music is so undisputed?"

More followed. The granddaughter of a famous sanxian artist, Ji Lao, also shared the same Weibo:

"My grandma wants me to ask sister @XiangYiV: are you interested in leaving the entertainment industry? Come teach at our school. ~The compensation will be very good heh~"

The netizens on the frontlines digesting the gossip began to check the information.

The folk music academy under the name of Ji Lao was small in scale, but it was one of the most desirable colleges for domestic folk music students.

The netizens all took a deep breath.

Then, an erhu performer also posted a Weibo hinting at a job offer: [Little Miss, it seems to me your sanxian is good enough. How about learning the erhu with me?]

The netizens took another deep breath.

A certain suona** master also posted in light spirits: [Why not learn the suona with me? You can blow it from the full moon to the first seventh quarter. You'll never be afraid of unemployment again.]

The netizens: F*ck, they were laughing in anger.

Then the official blog of the Chinese National Music Association forwarded a Weibo with their stamp of approval. [The young girl is promising, and there is hope in passing on the tradition. Today, the young generation of China shines with pride like the rising sun!]

It was not an exaggeration to say the whole folk music industry was shaken up by this remark!

Netizens: ….???!!!!

Could it be that we had misunderstood the Vase Fairy?

Did she really choose to enter the entertainment industry like a person from the national sports team who volunteers to visit the countryside and help the poor?

Thousands of words later, there was only one final concluding statement—

F*ck. What a badass!

Translator's Note:

*The Chinese word for turtle dove is Ban Jiu, which just happens to be the same character as Ban Hui's last name, Ban. Hence, his Weibo handle is a pun on his real name.

**Suona is another traditional Chinese instrument, similar to a very high pitched horn.

chapters.title: Chapter 57 - "Heartbeat" Goes Live!
chapters.body:
Chapter 57 - "Heartbeat" Goes Live!

…

The next day.

At 7:30, Li Jianyu sat half asleep, rubbing his eyes. They were in the garden with the planning department and the cameramen to discuss the day's arrangements.

They thought they had gotten up early enough until they saw Xiang Yi appear in the camera.

Li Jianyu greeted her in a friendly manner. "Good morning, mentor Xiang Yi. Are you going out to exercise?"

Xiang Yi gave him an odd look and politely said, "Good morning. I've just finished my morning exercise."

Everyone: ???

Li Jianyu: "Running? Next time, we can go together. I can run five kilometers, you know."

As a director, the most important thing to maintain was his health. Moreover, he liked photography, which meant he needed a lot of strength to carry a gadget weighing a dozen kilograms for a few hours.

Xiang Yi: "No, taichi. I also practice swordsmanship occasionally."

Everyone: ???

Is it the taichi that the grandpas and grandmas practice in the park?

Li Jianyu smiled dryly. "Mentor Xiang Yi, you're really… healthy."

Xiang Yi: "Hungry? We can eat."

"Ah?"

"I made breakfast for you." Xiang Yi glanced at the time on her sports wristband watch. "About time. It's the same number of people as yesterday, right? There's enough food for everyone."

Everyone onsite was flattered, and someone politely said, "Mentor Xiang Yi, you have to be on stream during the day, yet you're cooking for us… Is it too much work for you…"

"Not at all." Placidly, the girl said, "It's quite convenient for me, really. If you enjoy it, please do eat more, and feel free to let me know ahead of time if there's something you're craving."

She enjoyed the state of being fully engaged when cooking, which allowed her entire heart and being to calm down.

To others it might be tiring, but to her it was both a hobby and an exercise. What's not to love?

Five minutes later.

The entire cast and crew of "Two Under One Roof" ate their breakfast in a trance.

Red bean with black rice porridge, fresh meat stuffed buns, three-vegetable buns, and unlimited side dishes. There was also a bunch of delicately made wontons that could be cooked by anyone who wanted some, matched with some seaweed and dried shrimp. It was so fresh that people almost lost their tongues.

During breakfast, not a person spoke up—it was so delicious, they didn't even have time to gobble it up. Who would care to talk?

After a while.

"Burrrp…"

Satisfied hiccups sounded one after another.

"Aiya, I'll go do the dishes…"

"Then I'll go help Mentor Xiang Yi sweep the floor."

"I'll go clean the kitchen! And take out the trash!"

Li Jianyu felt the corners of his mouth twitch. He had never seen these people so active during work hours, yet now they were fighting for chores one by one!

'All they care about is food! No shame!'

'But since we're here, I'll just have another bowl of wontons. Just one!'

The marketing team of "Two Under One Roof" announced on Weibo that the stream would begin at 9:00 am sharp.

When the "Heartbeat" crew saw this announcement, they decided to change their scheduled time to air in advance.

"We're starting at 8:30!" Director Chen said with a gloomy look. "Today, no matter what, we're going to destroy "Two Under One Roof" once and for all! It's just cooking, right? Is Qin Wanyan ready?"

chapters.title: Chapter 58 - Wild Boar Can'T Digest Fine Sugar
chapters.body:
Chapter 58 - Wild Boar Can'T Digest Fine Sugar

"Miss Qin is prepared to make ham and egg sandwiches as well as handmade coffee later." His assistant was cautious and solemn, trying not to anger him. "This was a menu that was finalized through repeated research. It looks delicate and exquisite on camera and is much more suitable than Xiang Yi's stir-fry and peasant stew!"

Director Chen looked sullen as he sneered coldly, "That Li Jianyu is just a peasant. He doesn't know how to capture what the netizen wants. If people wanted to watch cooking, they would just go to a food broadcast. People want to see celebrities! His show is like a farmhouse. After wasting such a good venue, it really proves true that mountain boars can't eat fine sugar."

"Yes yes yes, Director Chen, you are right."

Eight thirty.

Muyao TV was good at marketing and had invited a full cast of popular stars. All of their fans were watching.

As soon as the clock ticked, the broadcast channels became full of people who immediately took over the comment section.

—[Jiang Chen, protector of all that's good in the world]

—[Wuli goddess Qin, go go go! It must be hard for you to descend from heaven today]

—[The princess has come! My prince Fu Jiayan in "Emperor's Way" was so manly!]

—[Yu Miaomiao, the gymnast who can be both cute and s.e.xy, everyone!]

The four celebrities greeted everyone simply. Now that there were handsome men and beautiful girls online, alongside Muyao TV's superior equipment and lighting, the broadcast was exceptionally high definition with crisp sound. It was an audio and visual feast.

The fans were excited beyond belief and frantically began to reward them.

Five minutes after the broadcast had gone live, the number of viewers as well as the "heartbeats" donated by fans both reached an all-time high in Jiang Chen's personal broadcast room.

He was followed by Fu Jiayan, Qin Wanyan, then Yu Miaomiao.

Qin Wanyan then asked everyone if they had breakfast. Everyone unanimously answered no. Qi Wanyi then brushed her hair back facing the camera and smiled gently, "Then I'll make something for everyone."

The live comments: [Wow, my goddess Qin actually knows how to cook?!]

Although the kitchen in "Heartbeat" could not be compared with the one from "Two Under One Roof", it was spacious and bright, and had a simple and modern design that made it very high-end.

Qin Wanyan did know how to cook. Her ancestors used to be imperial chefs, and her uncle also owned a very well-known food chain in the country.

From a young age, she had studied abroad and gradually developed a western palette. She didn't know how to make Chinese food, but she was quite skilled in western cuisine.

In a retro frying pan, she fried the eggs and ham while placing two thick slabs of toast in a toaster. Together, with a thick layer of tomato sauce, veggies, eggs and ham, she formed a delectable ham and egg sandwich.

Immediately afterward, Qin Wanyan began to slowly brew coffee.

She ground the coffee into powder and ran it through a filter. Her fair and slender fingers then held a gooseneck pot for the pour over. The scene was elegant and noble, as Qin Wanyan gently narrated the skills required to make coffee. "The coffee has to be ground into a uniform texture. Based on the characteristics of different coffee beans, the water temperature has to be properly adjusted. Usually, it should be kept at around 85-95 degrees Celsius…"

The fans in Qin Wanyan's personal broadcast enjoyed her talk very much, as if they were talking to their idol themselves and taking part in the activity.

—[Goddess Qin is so gentle! She's so nimble with her hands, but she's so patient too!]

—[The sandwiches and the coffee look amazing! It would look amazing posted on WeChat moments]

—[Didn't the short clip of that Vase Fairy cooking go viral yesterday? When I saw it, I felt that her skills were not bad, but it's a bit too distant. She's not even half as good as our Qin goddess!]

Then—

A passer-by in the main broadcast slowly raised the question:

chapters.title: Chapter 59 - Children Who Behave Will Be Rewarded
chapters.body:
Chapter 59 - Children Who Behave Will Be Rewarded

—[I would rather eat a burger from a certain Kaifeng dish rather than eat that sandwich…]

—[Is she trying to compete with the Vase Fairy? Although the Vase Fairy is annoying, at least her food looks appetizing. This Qin Wanyan's skills tho emmm…]

Director Chen had been staring at his phone the entire time, reading the netizens' comments. He couldn't understand it.

Someone, in particular, had posted: [Next door at "Two Under One Roof", they're starting to fry the milk! Friends, I will take off first!]

Whoosh whoosh whoosh.

The number of viewers in the stream began to decline noticeably.

Director Chen stared in disbelief.

How did it end up like this?!

He opened the stream for "Two Under One Roof". The stream had just begun, but the number of viewers was no less than "Heartbeat".

The milk curds had been prepared in advance. Mixing cornflour with fresh milk, salt and sugar to taste, it had been boiled over a slow fire before it was placed in the refrigerator to freeze.

The girl was once again in natural makeup with a calm expression. It was as if all the noise in the world couldn't sway her from concentrating on the ingredients in front of her.

The delicate white milk curds were dipped in flour, baking powder, yeast, and MSG. It formed a beautiful parabola as it was dropped into the fryer, happily swimming through the oil.

Xiang Yi's actions were swift, and soon, she had fried an entire pot. After scooping them out, she garnished it with some white sugar.

Li Jianyu picked up one and, in mutual understanding, the cameraman approached to do a closeup take of the fried milk.

The surface of the milk was a light yellow, and it was exquisitely crunchy. It came apart with a 'crunch'. The custard inside was smooth and soft, and if you squeezed the crispy crust with your hands a few times, the custard would slightly sway like jello.

The comments:

—[Ah ah ah I want some!!!]

—[I can smell the goodness through the screen]

—[She only fried one plate? This milk lover could eat an entire bucket by themselves!]

After brushing his teeth and washing his face, the first thing Yan Nai did was visit the kitchen.

The young boy's hair had been ruffled all night and looked like a messy bird's nest.

He approached Xiang Yi. "Big Sister, is it done?"

Xiang Yi blocked the camera for him. "Children with messy hair are not allowed at breakfast."

During yesterday's chat, Director Yan had revealed the news that he had wanted Yan Nai to debut in the entertainment industry. She didn't care about her appearances, but her beautiful little brother had to watch his image in front of the camera to avoid having his photos exploited for suspicious purposes.

Yan Nai paused for a moment and shyly shook his head. "I… I don't know how to style hair. Yesterday, the gege from the hairdressers styled my hair for me."

Xiang Yi thought for a moment. "Go find Ah Nan, he's pretty good with hair."

"Oh, okay."

"Wait."

"Hmm?"

Yan Nai turned around to a piece of fried milk being stuffed into his mouth. The gentle voice of the girl coaxed:

"Children who behave will be rewarded."

Slurp—

Yan Nai forgot how to chew as a flush of crimson spread from the tip of his ears to his neck.

'She fed me…'

'Does she li…like me…'

The young boy felt his brain become overwhelmed by his heartbeat and flushing cheeks. Hurriedly, he scurried out of the kitchen, bumping into a slender figure.

It was Shi Sui.

"Watch where you're going," he warned.

Yan Nai looked around, and seeing that there were no cameramen around, he cautiously whispered, delighted:

"Uncle, the big sister fed me food!"

chapters.title: Chapter 60 - Displeased
chapters.body:
Chapter 60 - Displeased

The handsome man's indifferent expression did not change, only raising an eyebrow. His peach blossom eyes seemed to be smiling but not, just like a sly old fox. "Was it good?"

The innocent boy shyly replied, "I ate it too fast and swallowed it before I could get a taste." Shi Sui's maturity and calmness made Yan Nai subconsciously feel that he could trust him with his ramblings. "I think big sister is so gentle. She blocked the camera for me and told me to go find her manager so I could get my hair done."

Shi Sui smiled and patted his head.

Yan Nai grinned. "Uncle, you're very gentle too!"

Shi Sui smiled kindly. "Uncle? Am I that old?"

"Huh?"

"Aiyah, I'm only twenty-seven and already an uncle. Next year, you might have to call me grandpa." Shi Sui sighed ruefully.

Yan Nai hurriedly waved his hands. "No no, uncle, you're not old at all. No no no, I meant big brother! Big brother Shi Sui!"

A certain fox did not feel guilt for trolling children and gently said, "Go ahead and get your hair done, make sure you look extra handsome before you go on camera."

"Okay, thank you unc… Big brother!"

He didn't know if it was an illusion, but Yan Nai certainly felt that the way Shi Sui looked at him after being called big brother was inexplicably a lot… kinder??

The cameras immediately turned toward Shi Sui as he entered the kitchen.

The man had a very good camera awareness and slightly curled his lips. "Good morning."

The stream's chat exploded.

—[Good morning, gege!]

—[Good morning, hubby!]

—[Whoever called him hubby, watch yourself. This is my man, OK? /dog /dog]

Xiang Yi looked up to meet his gaze.

"Morning." Shi Sui's lips curved even more.

"Morning."

Shi Sui approached her with his long legs. "Do you need help?"

"It's fine. Breakfast is ready. Fried milk with fresh meat wontons and veggies. I'm going to squeeze some juice for the child in a bit. What would Senior like to drink?"

Shi Sui helped her place the food on a wooden tray with familiar ease. There was no trace of pretense.

"Can I still put in a request without a little red flower?"

Xiang Yi was startled. She just remembered now that she had coaxed Shi Sui like a zaizai. If he collected five flowers, he could place an order.

She chuckled. She hadn't expected him to take that sentence seriously. "Senior, you have special privileges."

Shi Sui: "Then coffee?"

Xiang Yi nodded. "Okay, I'll go make the fruit juice."

Shi Sui lowered his eyelids and took the initiative to help bring out breakfast.

She's going to squeeze juice for Yan Nai first…

He was… displeased.

Inside the stream for "Heartbeat".

Qin Wanyan had finished prepping breakfast.

The live broadcast room was overflowing with question marks—because she had only made one sandwich.

—[How does this work? Are four a.d.u.l.ts going to share this one sandwich??]

The four people cut the sandwich into equal pieces and washed it down with a small cup of coffee. That concluded breakfast.

Yu Miamiao didn't even finish her portion, putting it down after only completing two-thirds. Apologetically, she said: "I'm going to be on set soon, the director told me to lose weight."

Although she was a gymnast, Yu Miaomiao resided in a semi-retired state. Currently, her main career path was developing in the entertainment industry.

Jiang Chen had a pretty cool personality, but he was very diligent. After eating, he took the initiative to go clean the dishes.

Fu Jiayan was much more flamboyant, as he praised Qin Wanyan's breakfast to no ends. "Wanyan is too good at cooking, it makes sense that she has studied abroad. I feel like I'm vacationing in the Seine."

The live comments:

—[Is this the real appetite of celebrities???]

—[Maybe for the female stars, sure, but two grown men only eating this little for breakfast…?]

—[Everyone everyone! They're starting breakfast next door! There's fried milk and fresh juice, and even the wontons were made from fresh meat filling!]

chapters.title: Chapter 61 - Can This Be Compared?
chapters.body:
Chapter 61 - Can This Be Compared?

All of a sudden, the netizens of 'Heartbeat' were shaken to the core.

On one side was a bland, tasteless sandwich, and on the other was a crispy, golden fried milk…

On one end was an extremely bitter black coffee, the other side a fresh, sweet, and refreshing juice…

F*ck!

Only children make choices. A.d.u.l.ts, of course, can just watch on their phone and their tablets!

Several netizens decided to do just that.

In the broadcast room for "Two Under One Roof".

The deep-fried milk was rich in creamy texture, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. A crisp crunch resounded after each bite, with the milk custard instantly melting the next second. It could be called a numbingly beautiful taste, and one could finish each in a bite or two, then pick up some vegetable dishes on the side to dull the grease after feeling full.

The steaming wontons were full and round, bathed in a soup base of chicken broth. After skimming the oil, a small handful of shrimp skin and seaweed was sprinkled in.

The juice was simple orange juice, lightly flavored with honey. Served in a transparent glass, it was beautiful and delicious.

Even if he repeatedly told himself not to eat too much for breakfast, Yan Zhenhua still ended up stuffing himself…

'Hehe. It seems he won't be losing weight, ever.'

Yan Nai could not stop his mouth either. The youth ate two bowls of wontons, his nose brimming with sweat drops.

The live comments:

—[Now that's what I call a meal!! Delicious!!]

—[I don't think sister Xiang Yi's cooking skills need to be praised further. Look how clean Yan Nai-didi's bowl is 233333]

—[Why isn't Shi Yingdi eating? Is it not to his tastes??]

—[Shisui doesn't like to eat breakfast, it's an ongoing problem. If he eats too much, he will also feel sick. His fans have been distressing over this for years Q_Q]

Some netizens were still unsatisfied. [Wasn't the Vase Fairy going to make coffee? Qin Wanyan over there already made hand-brewed coffee. She's not planning to make a cup of instant coffee for Shi Yingdi, right?]

As soon as this comment floated by, the camera in the main room cut to a scene in the villa.

—[?????]

—[WTF? Why is she so professional???!!!!]

—[Hehe, does the Vase Fairy know how to use such high-end equipment? Can she even afford to pay if she breaks it??]

With a comfortable expression, Xiang Yi began to grind the beans in a calm manner. She then poured the freshly ground coffee beans into the silver grips of the espresso machine, extracting the dark brown liquid.

Immediately, fans of Qin Wanyan who had joined after hearing this news became confused.

Where the f*ck did they get a professional espresso machine just now??

Even a challenger player boosting a bronzie needs to sign in, okay!!

A fan insisted: [Heh. Our Qin goddess made it by hand, that's real technique and skill. The Vase Fairy used an automated machine. How can these even be compared? We all know whose face is hurting from being slapped so hard!]

The live comments began to float—

With seemingly no effort at all, she used the steam tube and whipped the milk into a dense foam. The girl's white and slender hands then elegantly poured the foam into the coffee cup, shaking her wrists high and low. In the blink of an eye, she had poured out the shape of a lifelike kitten.

The netizens: !!!!

—[Latte art this good from just a quick whip?]

—[You call this casual? Go to a cafe and ask anyone to make this, and you will know the truth]

—[Ehehehe, one sentence to a certain fan: Can this be compared? We all know whose face is hurting from being slapped so hard!]

Qin Wanyan's fans suddenly felt a burning pain on their faces from the impact!

chapters.title: Chapter 62 - It Is My Honor To Serve You, Miss Xiang Yi 1
chapters.body:
Chapter 62 - It Is My Honor To Serve You, Miss Xiang Yi 1

Xiang Yi was crisp and efficient in her work, and very quickly, she had made a few more cups of coffee.

Concentrated espresso, iced americano, caramel macchiato… Nothing seemed difficult to her.

After snapping out of his trance, Li Jianyu began to interview her: "Mentor Xiang Yi, did you hold a barista qualification certificate? I hear it's quite difficult to obtain. You need theoretical knowledge of practical operations or something…"

Xiang Yi shook her head innocently.

The live comments: [I suspect the Vase Fairy's gaze is saying: Does this even need to be learned?]

Yet, when the girl opened her mouth, her tone was gentle and polite. "No, I do not; I just learned a bit here and there. My skills should be very average."

The live comments: [????????]

"By the way, if any of your staff want coffee, just come grab it for yourself. If you don't know how to use this semi-automatic espresso machine, there's a capsule machine over there. You can put the capsule in and coffee will automatically come out."

Li Jianyu was superficially polite. "No, no, mentor Xiang Yi, you're really too kind."

"It's nothing, really. These machines in my house will malfunction if they are not used for long durations of time."

Xiang Yi didn't like coffee very much, even with milk and sugar. She had purchased these machines for her brothers…

Thinking of them, Xiang Yi's gaze softened.

—[The Vase Fairy really can't take a compliment; this fakeness is too low level]

—[When her mouth is closed: a pretty girl. When she opens her mouth: a brainless vase. I recommend the TV crew to stop equipping Xiang Yi with a microphone. Can you just mute her?]

—[The coffee machine is from the brand Qcoffee, an internationally acclaimed brand. This vintage red color is also a limited edition machine, so even secondhand, it would cost over 80,000 RMB. Combined with the other bits and pieces here, there is at least 300,000 RMB worth of equipment in this room alone. I was reluctant to believe these machines belong to you in the first place, but do you really need to bring this stuff from home just to film a variety show? This is laughable.]

Xiang Yi brought three cups of coffee over.

Shi Sui chose the cup of espresso and raised his eyebrows slightly after a sip. Afterwards, he took several sips in a row, obviously enjoying the flavor.

Yan Zhenhua tasted the iced americano and casually praised, "The beans are great, Little Xiang Yi; you are an expert at picking them indeed."

Yan Nai looked at the remaining cup of coffee on the table, then back toward Xiang Yi. He wasn't sure if this cup was for him, or if Xiang Yi had made it for herself.

The young boy's eyes were glistening, and his hair blown out into a soft do by Ah Nan. He looked a bit like a little shota* from an anime.

Xiang Yi was much more patient with children when she was with other people and softly said, "This is made for you. I added a bit more caramel, so it shouldn't taste bitter."

Yan Nai's eyes lit up.

He picked up the coffee cup to take a look. The latte cat was cute and lovely, which made Yan Nai unable to resist showing off to Shi Sui.

"Big brother, look! Look, mine has a kitty."

Shi Sui's sipping motion paused.

'Don't get mad.'

'It's not worth it.'

'You don't care.'

Xiang Yi was only taking care of him like a child, nothing more.

"Meow~!" Just then, Xiao Naofu, who had been chasing butterflies in the garden, approached and jumped into Xiang Yi's arms.

As the girl rubbed the back of its neck, Xiao Naofu squinted its eyes in comfort and began to purr delicately.

Yan Zhenhua noted: "Why doesn't this cat eat kibble? I see it hasn't touched any of the food in its bowl."

"It's probably just picky with its food. I'm going to make some tuna and pumpkin cat food for it."

After she spoke, Xiang Yi couldn't help but kiss the little cat on its forehead.

Xiao Naofu: …!!

Its eyes gazed wide, with only one thought flowing through its head:

This tiger is no longer chaste!

Shi Sui's gaze, however, suddenly became a bit strange.

The girl had said that she saw him as… a cat?

*Translator's Note: Shota or ("zheng tai" in Chinese) refers to young, cute and soft-looking boys, similar to a little brother trope often seen in anime. This also refers to the ones who wear shorts in particular.

chapters.title: Chapter 63 - It Is My Honor To Serve You, Miss Xiang Yi 2
chapters.body:
Chapter 63 - It Is My Honor To Serve You, Miss Xiang Yi 2

After successfully kissing the cat, Xiang Yi revealed a satisfied expression. The corners of her lips curled up, her eyes glistening with stars.

—[Sister, you can use my face too!]

—[Who doesn't love that??]

—[Suddenly, I feel like the term "Vase Fairy" is not that derogatory…]

To her side, Yan Nai was stunned.

A sudden gust of wind blew over the climbing roses that had made their way onto the summer pavilion, their petals falling slowly. The scene was that of a slow-motion sequence in a movie.

When the girl wasn't smiling, she maintained a calm temperament. But when she smiled, it was bright and vigorous. Her side profile was delicate; her nose and lips were exquisite when forming a smile—bright and beautiful.

Yan Nai felt his heart pounding as his ears turned red.

Yan Zhenhua revealed a gratified smile of an old father as he aided his son, "Little Xiang Yi, we will be taking off at noon. Let us know what needs to be done, don't hold back. Just tell Ah Nai, and he'll take care of it!"

Yan Nai straightened his back immediately, sitting more proper than a primary school student in class.

"Director Yan and Ah Nai both love spicy food, how about I make you some chili oil to take home?" Xiang Yi suggested.

Yan Zhenhua responded well to the suggestion, feeling even more satisfied with Xiang Yi in his heart.

Even her gifts were delivered directly to his heart.

What a great girl. He can't let other stinky men take advantage of her.

Xiang Yi: "I have several kinds of chilis in my garden, so I'll pick a bit of each. Mixed together, the chili oil will taste even better."

The live comments:

—[There she goes again. I can't watch this anymore, I'm going to ditch this variety show]

—[Is watching Jiang Chen, Qin Wanyan, Fu Jiayan, and Yu Miaomiao fall in love not good enough for you? The Vase Fairy is too revolting]

—[Director, are you sure you don't want to replace her with someone else? Who are you trying to disgust by bringing such a celebrity on board?? What do you take Shi Yingdi for? As long as she gives you money, you'll let her be his partner??]

The comments were in disarray.

A staff member handed Li Jianyu his mobile phone. Seeing the comments, Li Jianyu felt his head inflate. Just as he was struggling to find the words to explain, a loud and hefty voice of a man appeared from a distance.

"Miss Yi, I'm here to deliver ingredients for you!"

Xiang Yi squeezed Xiao Naofu's chubby paw and softly responded, "Your fish is here, let's go get it?"

Xiao Naofu, who had tasted Xiang Yi's concoctions before, happily meowed in a self-assumed reserved manner.

Xiang Yi stood up and left the pavilion holding Xiao Naofu.

The man who was delivering the ingredients approached her with long strides. He was dark-skinned and had his hair in a basic buzz cut. Wearing blue overalls with the words "Little Rabbit Farm" embroidered on it, the man was carrying a bamboo basket and a cooler in his arms.

The live comments: [Little Rabbit Farm?? The one that sells their strawberries individually for over 12 RMB each??]

"Miss Yi, here's the tuna and pumpkin you requested. I also prepared some salted duck egg and soghua eggs for you. The milk here is also freshly squeezed."

"Thanks for your efforts, Uncle Wang."

"No worries, no worries. It's my honor to serve you, Miss Xiang Yi!" The man broke into a goofy smile. "Don't worry about it, I'll help you put it in the kitchen!"

—[Is this an actor hired by the crew? Trying a bit too hard aren't we…]

—[Are my eyes going blind? Why does this guy look so much like the boss of Little Rabbit Farm…]

—[I just searched this from an online encyclopedia. His name is Wang Shunli, male, age 41, and the CEO of Little Rabbit Farm. Wang Shunli is a well-known farmer and entrepreneur and has even won the Citizen Medal awarded by the mayor of Nancheng City. Why is such a big boss s.u.c.k.i.n.g up to Xiang Yi like a pathetic dog??]

chapters.title: Chapter 64 - The Luxurious Mansion Belongs To The Vase Fairy?!
chapters.body:
Chapter 64 - The Luxurious Mansion Belongs To The Vase Fairy?!

—[I…I have an unrealistic thought… What if this house really belongs to Xiang Yi…]

—[You must be the Vase Fairy's fan right? If you know it's unrealistic, don't say it in the first place, okay? Are you not afraid of people laughing their asses off]

—[Aiyaya, the navy* is late, but never absent. How much money did the Vase Fairy pay you to write that comment? If there are money-making opportunities, we should be in it together!]

All the comments in the stream channel were full of cynicism against Xiang Yi.

After organizing the ingredients, Wang Shunli also took out the garbage as he was leaving.

"Thank you, Uncle Wang." Xiang Yi said.

Wang Shunli shook his head. "What's there to thank me for? It's on the way. If you want to talk about gratitude, I should be the one thanking you. If it weren't for you, ruffians like us who only know how to plow lands wouldn't be able to live such a good life now." He sharply patted his forehead. "Look at my memory, I almost forgot about the gifts I'd prepared for everyone."

He took out what seemed to be a remote control and pressed it twice. Six circular-shaped robots came driving in from the direction of the front gate. Each robot was pulling a small cart with several foam boxes stacked on top of each other.

Wang Shunli: "Miss. Yi, I heard you've been streaming lately, and a newly joined young man from our farm mentioned that on live broadcasts, one must give out gifts to their fans. We can't let you be embarrassed! Here is what we've prepared for you!"

He casually opened one of the containers. Its inside was jam-packed with several boxes full of fresh strawberries, bright red in color, and each the size of half a palm.

He then opened another box: vacuum-packed spicy rabbit heads in bags weighing over two jin each. It was a hefty portion with no excess packaging.

There were also handmade beef balls, egg yolk crisps, and other specialty food made at the Little Rabbit Farm.

The netizens in the live broadcast room went into a frenzy.

—[He's willing to just give away strawberries that cost 12 bucks a piece??]

—[As I recall, those spicy rabbit heads are sold in limited numbers of fifty a week. They're already worth hundreds in the black market! That bag looks massive!!]

—[I'm going to faint: handmade beef balls are my favorite. QQQQ beef balls from other restaurants don't taste nearly as good as the ones from Little Rabbit Farm. So what if it costs a bit more? I can afford it if I grit my teeth. But the limited amount is enough to make a person hopeless]

Xiang Yi sweeped her gaze over the gifts and nodded her head lightly. "Alright, these will do." As she spoke, she turned to Li Jianyu with an innocent gaze. "How should we hand these out? Lottery?"

Li Jianyu was also dumbfounded.

Is the world of the new rich really so unpretentious and down to earth??

Li Jianyu had blanked out for a long time. It was the cameraman, Lao Li, who ended up kicking him out of his trance. Li Jianyu reacted and cleared his throat. "Then I will take it to the official marketing team for them to engage the lottery."

Wang Shunli stared at Xiang Yi. He opened his mouth, but no sound came out even when he had something to say.

Xiang Yi: "Uncle Wang, what's wrong?"

Wang Shunli couldn't help but say what was in his heart. "Miss Yi, I heard you weren't making very much money in the entertainment industry. When will you get bored of it and come back to inherit our Little Rabbit Farm?"

"Also, I keep seeing the irresponsible remarks the netizens have been making regarding you. But this is clearly your home!" Wang Shunli said indignantly. "I almost feel wronged on your behalf!"

The live comments: [?????!!!!!]

'What did you say?'

'The Vase Fairy owns Little Rabbit Farm?'

'This luxurious mansion belongs to the Vase Fairy, too?!'

*Translator's note: The term 'navy' translates literally to 'water warriors', kind of like white knights, but in this case, these are online users who seem to be sent by a specific party or company to either dispel or propagate rumors, or act as damage control/PR.

chapters.title: Chapter 65 - Miss Bai Fumei
chapters.body:
Chapter 65 - Miss Bai Fumei

The netizens were speechless from the shock.

The anti-fans of Xiang Yi, who had been flaming her for being 'uncultured', has no 'f*cking clue' in particular. But here was proof that what she has been calling her house is really her house!

A few seconds of silence passed by.

—[Can I ask weakly, what the hell is a Little Rabbit Farm?]

—[I'm from Nancheng, so let me explain this popular science. Little Rabbit Farm is the pride and joy of our southern city. The farm used to be on a wasteland, and the farmers in the surrounding area were very poor as well. Then, some big boss invested in high-tech farming, such as cultivating high-quality seeds, introducing automatic seeders and harvesters, and automated stock retrieval. Although the total output is not high, the quality was able to reach the level of specialty supplied goods! A small number of semi-processed foods can also be purchased online, but remember, all the other products can only be bought locally in Nancheng!]

—[Online shopping relies on your hand speed, while offline shopping relies on how fast you run. Yesterday, I was only a second late and missed out on the last box of strawberries in my supermarket from Little Rabbit Farm. /goodbye /goodbye]

Some netizens went off to a search engine and looked up Little Rabbit Farm. The price that first popped up were the listed prices of the agricultural projects. The numbers were enough to make people wonder if the merchant had input the wrong number.

Little Rabbit Farm specialty strawberries: 12 RMB/piece, starting at 6 pieces. Shipping not included.

?????

You can buy a pound of strawberries with 12 RMB!

Upon cl.i.c.k.i.n.g into the comment section, one could see the buyers' high praise, describing their taste as heavenly. Even ass-kissing comments going as far as saying "these berries should only exist in heaven" constantly popped out.

Heh? Is it really that good?

I don't believe it!

Let's buy one and try it out!

Just as they were confirming the purchase, a prompt popped up:

This product is sold out.

Motherf*cker, is this how you run a business? I'm trying to spend money, but you won't even let me do that??

Cl.i.c.k.i.n.g into the other products, one could see they were all equally absurd in price. Yet, they were all fascinatingly seductive and were all sold out without exception.

You think I'd fall for such a low-end consumer trap?!

…Motherf*cker. Alright, if I can't buy directly, then to the black market it is!

With another click, the black market prices were as listed below:

Second-hand/authentic/Free e-shipping/Little Rabbit Farm Specialty Strawberries: 36 RMB/pc, 6 pcs minimum. Bought based on own ability. Does not accept negotiations or unresponsive buyers. If you want it, click buy. I was only able to get two boxes, so suit yourself.

???

Are all second-hand sellers this arrogant these days??

After such drama, several netizens began to form an intuitive understanding of Little Rabbit Farm:

Expensive.

Hard to buy.

See you at the Weibo lottery!

However, the majority of netizens had forgotten the Little Rabbit Farm for the time being. The film location being used for "Two Under One Roof" was actually the Vase Fairy's home?

This heavenly mansion, with its fairytale garden, vast vegetable fields, clear ponds, far off farms… All of it belonged to the Vase Fairy?!

Countless netizens fell into a state of contemplation.

Her entering the entertainment industry was the equivalent of a national team player going to the countryside to help the poor.

She was clearly a Bai Fumei*, engaged in the temporary process of experiencing the suffering of real life!!

*Translator's note: Bai Fumei = Term used to describe someone who is respectively fair Skinned, wealthy, and beautiful

chapters.title: Chapter 66 - The Peak Of Bullsh*Tting
chapters.body:
Chapter 66 - The Peak Of Bullsh*Tting

The spring pavilion, Xiang estates.

Facing Uncle Wang's inquiries, Xiang Yi's fair face seemed at a loss. "What are the netizens saying?"

Based on her assumptions, she shouldn't have a presence on the variety show at all.

Uncle Wang opened his mouth. "You… haven't read the comments?"

Xiang Yi responded softly: "My eyes are being treated, so I haven't been using my phone."

Her eyesight used to be very good; but it seems this year the person occupying her body had overused her eyes, causing her to be slightly nearsighted.

Xiang Yi was unable to adapt and ended up taking the initiative herself by putting together a medicated diet and Traditional Chinese Medicine eye masks. To ensure the effect, she had to reduce the use of all electronic products.

Uncle Wang's voice was shaking with anger. "Those netizens are overboard. They said you're a magpie in a dove's nest, pretending to be the master of someone else's house! This is obviously your home, the home that you personally cleaned up yourself…"

"Is that so…" Xiang Yi nodded. Her expression didn't have any notable changes and seemed overall indifferent to the netizens' comments.

Shi Sui lazily leaned against the back of his chair, tapping the armrest with his fingers.

The Yan father and son next to him looked just as shocked.

This place… is Xiang Yi's home?

Director Yan suddenly had the feeling his little cub was not good enough for this little rich woman…

Li Jianyu hurriedly cleared his throat. Initially, Xiang Yi had mentioned there was no need to tell the netizens that this was her home, but since the anti-fans kept provoking the issue, even a Buddha would get angry sometimes.

Now that the weight has been lifted, he had to continue this face-slapping fiesta, even if the girl was being very low-key!

"It's like this. Everyone probably knows we had an incident before our live broadcast began. The film location was maliciously exposed, which seriously impacted our ability to shoot on time.
